item.title: Thermal Uhlmann-Chern number: Bridging pure and mixed states
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Wang, Xu-Yang Hou, Yan He, and Hao Guo

Topological properties of quantum systems at finite temperatures, described by mixed states, pose significant challenges due to the triviality of the Uhlmann bundle. By inserting the density matrix into the Chern character, we introduce the thermal Uhlmann-Chern number, a generalization of the Chern…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214112] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Wang, Xu-Yang Hou, Yan He, and Hao Guo

Topological properties of quantum systems at finite temperatures, described by mixed states, pose significant challenges due to the triviality of the Uhlmann bundle. By inserting the density matrix into the Chern character, we introduce the thermal Uhlmann-Chern number, a generalization of the Chern…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214112] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Thermal Uhlmann-Chern number: Bridging pure and mixed states
item.dc:creator: Xin Wang, Xu-Yang Hou, Yan He, and Hao Guo
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Structure, structural phase transitions, mechanical properties, defects

item.title: Revealing quantum phase string effect in a doped Mott insulator: A tensor network state study
item.description:
Author(s): Wayne Zheng, Jia-Xin Zhang, Zheng-Yuan Yue, Zheng-Cheng Gu, and Zheng-Yu Weng

We apply the fermionic tensor network (TN) state method to understand the strongly correlated nature in a doped Mott insulator. We conduct a comparative study of the $σt\text{−}J$ model, in which the no-double-occupancy constraint remains unchanged but the quantum phase string effect associated with…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214514] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wayne Zheng, Jia-Xin Zhang, Zheng-Yuan Yue, Zheng-Cheng Gu, and Zheng-Yu Weng

We apply the fermionic tensor network (TN) state method to understand the strongly correlated nature in a doped Mott insulator. We conduct a comparative study of the σt−J model, in which the no-double-occupancy constraint remains unchanged but the quantum phase string effect associated with doped ho…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214514] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Revealing quantum phase string effect in a doped Mott insulator: A tensor network state study
item.dc:creator: Wayne Zheng, Jia-Xin Zhang, Zheng-Yuan Yue, Zheng-Cheng Gu, and Zheng-Yu Weng

item.title: Unveiling the origin of size-dependent topological zero eigenmodes via singular-value decomposition
item.description:
Author(s): San-Ren He, Jing Fan, Dong-Hui Xu, Lin Li, and Zhen-Hua Wang

Recent studies have reported a puzzling phenomenon in non-Hermitian systems: topological zero eigenmodes (TZMs) appear and disappear as the system size varies, seemingly contradicting the inherent robustness of topology. This work demonstrates that these observations do not signify genuine topologic…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224516] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): San-Ren He, Jing Fan, Dong-Hui Xu, Lin Li, and Zhen-Hua Wang

Recent studies have reported a puzzling phenomenon in non-Hermitian systems: topological zero eigenmodes (TZMs) appear and disappear as the system size varies, seemingly contradicting the inherent robustness of topology. This work demonstrates that these observations do not signify genuine topologic…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224516] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Unveiling the origin of size-dependent topological zero eigenmodes via singular-value decomposition
item.dc:creator: San-Ren He, Jing Fan, Dong-Hui Xu, Lin Li, and Zhen-Hua Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Optical selection rules of topological excitons in flat bands
item.description:
Author(s): Mara Lozano, Hong-Yi Xie, and Bruno Uchoa

Topological excitons are superpositions of electron-hole pair states, characterized by an envelope function with finite vorticity in momentum space. This vorticity is determined by the underlying topology of the electronic bands. We derive the optical selection rules for topological excitons in flat…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235417] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mara Lozano, Hong-Yi Xie, and Bruno Uchoa

Topological excitons are superpositions of electron-hole pair states, characterized by an envelope function with finite vorticity in momentum space. This vorticity is determined by the underlying topology of the electronic bands. We derive the optical selection rules for topological excitons in flat…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235417] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Optical selection rules of topological excitons in flat bands
item.dc:creator: Mara Lozano, Hong-Yi Xie, and Bruno Uchoa

item.title: Impact of incident photon energy and pulse intensity on circularly polarized high harmonic generation in monolayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Yu-Ze Li, Ting-Ting Xi, Yu-Hui Song, Huan-Cheng Yang, Kai Liu, and Zhong-Yi Lu

Circularly polarized high harmonics provide important light sources for ultrafast detection of chiral molecules and solid materials. Although the circularly polarized high harmonic generation (HHG) has been reported in many solid materials, the comprehensive study about its dependence on the driving…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235418] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Ze Li, Ting-Ting Xi, Yu-Hui Song, Huan-Cheng Yang, Kai Liu, and Zhong-Yi Lu

Circularly polarized high harmonics provide important light sources for ultrafast detection of chiral molecules and solid materials. Although the circularly polarized high harmonic generation (HHG) has been reported in many solid materials, the comprehensive study about its dependence on the driving…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235418] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025

item.title: Fermi-liquid-like phase driven by next-nearest-neighbor couplings in a lightly doped kagome-lattice $t−J$ model
item.description:
Author(s): Xu-Yan Jia, Fan Yang, D. N. Sheng, and Shou-Shu Gong

Due to the interplay between charge fluctuation and geometry frustration, the doped kagome-lattice Mott insulator is a fascinating platform to realize exotic quantum states. Through the state-of-the-art density matrix renormalization group calculation, we explore the quantum phases of the lightly do…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245145] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xu-Yan Jia, Fan Yang, D. N. Sheng, and Shou-Shu Gong

Due to the interplay between charge fluctuation and geometry frustration, the doped kagome-lattice Mott insulator is a fascinating platform to realize exotic quantum states. Through the state-of-the-art density matrix renormalization group calculation, we explore the quantum phases of the lightly do…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245145] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Fermi-liquid-like phase driven by next-nearest-neighbor couplings in a lightly doped kagome-lattice $t−J$ model

item.title: From chiral topological dynamics to chiral topological amplification: Real versus imaginary parameters in a Hermitian bosonic chain
item.description:
Author(s): Kiran Babasaheb Estake, T. R. Vishnu, and Dibyendu Roy

We propose a Hermitian quadratic bosonic model (QBH) whose dynamical matrix exhibits distinct topological and dynamical phenomena depending on whether the hopping and pairing amplitudes are real or purely imaginary. In the real-parameter regime, the dynamical matrix is unitarily equivalent to four d…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214314] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kiran Babasaheb Estake, T. R. Vishnu, and Dibyendu Roy

We propose a Hermitian quadratic bosonic model (QBH) whose dynamical matrix exhibits distinct topological and dynamical phenomena depending on whether the hopping and pairing amplitudes are real or purely imaginary. In the real-parameter regime, the dynamical matrix is unitarily equivalent to four d…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214314] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025

item.title: Superconductivity in a Chern band: Effect of time-reversal symmetry breaking on superconductivity
item.description:
Author(s): Bernhard E. Lüscher and Mark H. Fischer

Time-reversal symmetry breaking is generally understood to be detrimental for superconductivity. However, recent experiments found superconductivity emerging out of a normal state showing a finite anomalous Hall effect, indicative of time-reversal symmetry breaking, in diverse systems from kagome me…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214513] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bernhard E. Lüscher and Mark H. Fischer

Time-reversal symmetry breaking is generally understood to be detrimental for superconductivity. However, recent experiments found superconductivity emerging out of a normal state showing a finite anomalous Hall effect, indicative of time-reversal symmetry breaking, in diverse systems from kagome me…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214513] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Superconductivity in a Chern band: Effect of time-reversal symmetry breaking on superconductivity

item.title: Interplay between Hubbard interaction and charge transfer energy in the three-orbital Emery model: Implications for cuprates and nickelates
item.description:
Author(s): Yan Peng and Mi Jiang

We use the numerically unbiased determinant quantum Monte Carlo method to systematically investigate the three-orbital Emery model in the normal state in a wide range of local interactions, charge transfer energy, and doping levels. We focus on the influence of the on-site Hubbard ${U}_{dd}$ and the…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235147] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan Peng and Mi Jiang

We use the numerically unbiased determinant quantum Monte Carlo method to systematically investigate the three-orbital Emery model in the normal state in a wide range of local interactions, charge transfer energy, and doping levels. We focus on the influence of the on-site Hubbard Udd and the charge…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235147] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025

item.title: Correlated Hopf insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Konstantinos Ladovrechis and Shouvik Sur

Hopf insulators represent an exceptional class of topological matter, unanticipated by the periodic table of topological invariants. These systems point to the existence of previously unexplored states of matter with unconventional topology. In this work, we take a step toward exploring this directi…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235148] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Konstantinos Ladovrechis and Shouvik Sur

Hopf insulators represent an exceptional class of topological matter, unanticipated by the periodic table of topological invariants. These systems point to the existence of previously unexplored states of matter with unconventional topology. In this work, we take a step toward exploring this directi…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235148] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025

item.title: Orientation dependent anomalous Hall and spin Hall currents at junctions of altermagnets with $p$-wave magnets
item.description:
Author(s): Sachchidanand Das and Abhiram Soori

We study charge and spin transport across a junction between an altermagnet (AM) and a $p$-wave magnet (PM) using a continuum model with boundary conditions tailored to the spin-split band structures of the two materials. Remarkably, although neither AM nor PM is spin-polarized, we find that the jun…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235309] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sachchidanand Das and Abhiram Soori

We study charge and spin transport across a junction between an altermagnet (AM) and a p-wave magnet (PM) using a continuum model with boundary conditions tailored to the spin-split band structures of the two materials. Remarkably, although neither AM nor PM is spin-polarized, we find that the junct…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235309] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Orientation dependent anomalous Hall and spin Hall currents at junctions of altermagnets with $p$-wave magnets

item.title: Quantum transport in the one-dimensional Hubbard model: Drude weights and Seebeck effect
item.description:
Author(s): Jia-Jia Luo, Sagarika Basak, Han Pu, and Xi-Wen Guan

The Drude weight (DW) is an essential quantity that characterizes the quantum transport properties of many-body systems. However, a rigorous understanding and exact computation of DWs, particularly for strongly correlated systems with doping, still remain elusive. In this article, taking advantage o…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235414] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jia-Jia Luo, Sagarika Basak, Han Pu, and Xi-Wen Guan

The Drude weight (DW) is an essential quantity that characterizes the quantum transport properties of many-body systems. However, a rigorous understanding and exact computation of DWs, particularly for strongly correlated systems with doping, still remain elusive. In this article, taking advantage o…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235414] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantum transport in the one-dimensional Hubbard model: Drude weights and Seebeck effect
item.dc:creator: Jia-Jia Luo, Sagarika Basak, Han Pu, and Xi-Wen Guan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Noise resilience of two-dimensional Floquet topological phases
item.description:
Author(s): Balaganchi A. Bhargava, Sanjib Kumar Das, Lukas M. Sieberer, and Ion Cosma Fulga

The authors show here that two-dimensional Floquet topological phases exhibit surprising resilience to timing noise, i.e., to the unavoidable deviations from perfectly periodic driving that occur in any experiment. Topological edge modes decay in two stages: a fast exponential decay at short times, due to thermalization among only edge modes and separate from the bulk, followed by a much slower, diffusive decay. These findings are supported by numerical simulations and by analytical results derived using a phenomenological model.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235416] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Balaganchi A. Bhargava, Sanjib Kumar Das, Lukas M. Sieberer, and Ion Cosma Fulga

The authors show here that two-dimensional Floquet topological phases exhibit surprising resilience to timing noise, i.e., to the unavoidable deviations from perfectly periodic driving that occur in any experiment. Topological edge modes decay in two stages: a fast exponential decay at short times, due to thermalization among only edge modes and separate from the bulk, followed by a much slower, diffusive decay. These findings are supported by numerical simulations and by analytical results derived using a phenomenological model.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235416] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Noise resilience of two-dimensional Floquet topological phases
item.dc:creator: Balaganchi A. Bhargava, Sanjib Kumar Das, Lukas M. Sieberer, and Ion Cosma Fulga
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Topological phase diagram of generalized Su-Schrieffer-Heeger models with interactions
item.description:
Author(s): Yuxiao Hang and Stephan Haas

We investigate interacting Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chains with two- and three-site unit cells using density matrix renormalization group (DMRG) simulations. By selecting appropriate filling fractions and sweeping across interaction strength ${J}_{z}$ and dimerization $δ$, we map out their phase d…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245138] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuxiao Hang and Stephan Haas

We investigate interacting Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chains with two- and three-site unit cells using density matrix renormalization group (DMRG) simulations. By selecting appropriate filling fractions and sweeping across interaction strength Jz and dimerization δ, we map out their phase diagrams a…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245138] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Topological phase diagram of generalized Su-Schrieffer-Heeger models with interactions

item.title: Entanglement and fluctuations of the fractonic phases in a one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbard chain
item.description:
Author(s): Chenrong Liu

We study the entanglement and bipartite fluctuations of fractonic phases in a one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbard chain using the density matrix renormalization group method. We demonstrate that bipartite charge fluctuations fail to detect quantum phase transitions in fractonic systems. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245139] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chenrong Liu

We study the entanglement and bipartite fluctuations of fractonic phases in a one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbard chain using the density matrix renormalization group method. We demonstrate that bipartite charge fluctuations fail to detect quantum phase transitions in fractonic systems. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245139] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Entanglement and fluctuations of the fractonic phases in a one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbard chain

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Non-Abelian fractional Chern insulator on a hyperbolic lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Ai-Lei He, Lu Qi, Wei-Wei Luo, and Yongjun Liu

The authors provide here convincing evidence for a non-Abelian fractional Chern insulator (FCI) state in a hyperbolic lattice. This state is characterized by the edge excitation spectra，the angular momentum of the ground state, and the trial wave functions constructed based on the generalized Pauli principle and Jack polynomials. A high value of the overlap between trial wave functions and exactly numerical results not only demonstrates the hyperbolic non-Abelian FCI state, but also reveals the geometric degree of freedom in this state.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245140] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ai-Lei He, Lu Qi, Wei-Wei Luo, and Yongjun Liu

The authors provide here convincing evidence for a non-Abelian fractional Chern insulator (FCI) state in a hyperbolic lattice. This state is characterized by the edge excitation spectra，the angular momentum of the ground state, and the trial wave functions constructed based on the generalized Pauli principle and Jack polynomials. A high value of the overlap between trial wave functions and exactly numerical results not only demonstrates the hyperbolic non-Abelian FCI state, but also reveals the geometric degree of freedom in this state.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245140] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Non-Abelian fractional Chern insulator on a hyperbolic lattice
item.dc:creator: Ai-Lei He, Lu Qi, Wei-Wei Luo, and Yongjun Liu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Electronic structure of monolayer ${\mathrm{CrTe}}_{2}$: An antiferromagnetic two-dimensional van der Waals material
item.description:
Author(s): Olivia Armitage, Naina Kushwaha, Akhil Rajan, Luke C. Rhodes, Sebastian Buchberger, Bruno Kenichi Saika, Shu Mo, Matthew D. Watson, Phil D. C. King, and Peter Wahl

By combining angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy with quasiparticle interference imaging, the authors establish here the electronic structure and magnetic ground state of monolayer CrTe2. Comparison with density functional theory calculations shows that inclusion of a moderate on-site Coulomb interaction is required to accurately capture the band structure. The work demonstrates how momentum- and real-space spectroscopies together resolve the electronic structure, magnetism and correlations in two-dimensional van der Waals materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245416] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Olivia Armitage, Naina Kushwaha, Akhil Rajan, Luke C. Rhodes, Sebastian Buchberger, Bruno Kenichi Saika, Shu Mo, Matthew D. Watson, Phil D. C. King, and Peter Wahl

By combining angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy with quasiparticle interference imaging, the authors establish here the electronic structure and magnetic ground state of monolayer CrTe2. Comparison with density functional theory calculations shows that inclusion of a moderate on-site Coulomb interaction is required to accurately capture the band structure. The work demonstrates how momentum- and real-space spectroscopies together resolve the electronic structure, magnetism and correlations in two-dimensional van der Waals materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245416] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Electronic structure of monolayer ${\mathrm{CrTe}}_{2}$: An antiferromagnetic two-dimensional van der Waals material
item.dc:creator: Olivia Armitage, Naina Kushwaha, Akhil Rajan, Luke C. Rhodes, Sebastian Buchberger, Bruno Kenichi Saika, Shu Mo, Matthew D. Watson, Phil D. C. King, and Peter Wahl
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Quantum criticality and emergent order in the spin-1 bilinear-biquadratic Kitaev chain
item.description:
Author(s): Zhiling Wei, Zhengzhong Du, Xiaodong Cao, Wen-Long You, and Yi Lu

Higher-spin materials can host exotic quantum phases with no counterparts in familiar spin-½ systems. Using density matrix renormalization group calculations, the authors chart here the phase diagram of a spin-1 bilinear-biquadratic-Kitaev chain and reveal two Kitaev-driven phases. A Kitaev nematic phase emerges from a delicate biquadratic-dimer background through an Ising quantum critical point, which further evolves into a Kitaev dimer phase that spontaneously breaks screw symmetry and crystallizes into a gapped order of alternating ℤ2 bond parities.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214434] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhiling Wei, Zhengzhong Du, Xiaodong Cao, Wen-Long You, and Yi Lu

Higher-spin materials can host exotic quantum phases with no counterparts in familiar spin-½ systems. Using density matrix renormalization group calculations, the authors chart here the phase diagram of a spin-1 bilinear-biquadratic-Kitaev chain and reveal two Kitaev-driven phases. A Kitaev nematic phase emerges from a delicate biquadratic-dimer background through an Ising quantum critical point, which further evolves into a Kitaev dimer phase that spontaneously breaks screw symmetry and crystallizes into a gapped order of alternating ℤ2 bond parities.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214434] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantum criticality and emergent order in the spin-1 bilinear-biquadratic Kitaev chain
item.dc:creator: Zhiling Wei, Zhengzhong Du, Xiaodong Cao, Wen-Long You, and Yi Lu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Linear magnetoresistance of two-dimensional massless Dirac fermions in the quantum limit
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao-Bin Qiang, Han-Yi Xu, Ren-Jie Tong, Shuai Li, Zi-Xuan Gao, Peng-Lu Zhao, and Hai-Zhou Lu

Linear magnetoresistance is a hallmark of three-dimensional (3D) Weyl metals in the quantum limit. Recently, a pronounced linear magnetoresistance has also been observed in 2D graphene [Xin et al., Nature (London) 616, 270 (2023)]. However, a comprehensive theoretical understanding remains elusive.…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224208] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao-Bin Qiang, Han-Yi Xu, Ren-Jie Tong, Shuai Li, Zi-Xuan Gao, Peng-Lu Zhao, and Hai-Zhou Lu

Linear magnetoresistance is a hallmark of three-dimensional (3D) Weyl metals in the quantum limit. Recently, a pronounced linear magnetoresistance has also been observed in 2D graphene [Xin et al., Nature (London) 616, 270 (2023)]. However, a comprehensive theoretical understanding remains elusive.…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224208] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.dc:title: Linear magnetoresistance of two-dimensional massless Dirac fermions in the quantum limit
item.dc:creator: Xiao-Bin Qiang, Han-Yi Xu, Ren-Jie Tong, Shuai Li, Zi-Xuan Gao, Peng-Lu Zhao, and Hai-Zhou Lu

item.title: Complex magnetic phase diagram of the metallic kagome ice HoAgGe
item.description:
Author(s): Jiyuan Li, Qihe Yu, Yifang Wang, Baopeng Song, Jiawen Zhang, Sai Yang, Yang Chen, Ye Chen, Zhen Ma, Shuhan Zheng, Yao Li, Meifeng Liu, Xiuzhang Wang, Saiyu Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Yu Song, and Huiqiu Yuan

HoAgGe crystallizes in a frustrated ZrNiAl-type structure with ${\mathrm{Ho}}^{3+}$ ions forming a distorted kagome lattice in the $ab$ plane, and is reported to host a crystalline kagome spin ice state, characterized by discrete degeneracies and distinct transport properties. Here, the field-temper…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224426] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiyuan Li, Qihe Yu, Yifang Wang, Baopeng Song, Jiawen Zhang, Sai Yang, Yang Chen, Ye Chen, Zhen Ma, Shuhan Zheng, Yao Li, Meifeng Liu, Xiuzhang Wang, Saiyu Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Yu Song, and Huiqiu Yuan

HoAgGe crystallizes in a frustrated ZrNiAl-type structure with Ho3+ ions forming a distorted kagome lattice in the ab plane, and is reported to host a crystalline kagome spin ice state, characterized by discrete degeneracies and distinct transport properties. Here, the field-temperature phase diagra…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224426] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.dc:title: Complex magnetic phase diagram of the metallic kagome ice HoAgGe
item.dc:creator: Jiyuan Li, Qihe Yu, Yifang Wang, Baopeng Song, Jiawen Zhang, Sai Yang, Yang Chen, Ye Chen, Zhen Ma, Shuhan Zheng, Yao Li, Meifeng Liu, Xiuzhang Wang, Saiyu Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Yu Song, and Huiqiu Yuan

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Superconductivity and band topology in the double-layer honeycomb structure compounds ${X}_{2}{\mathrm{C}}_{2}$ $(X=\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{Ta}, \mathrm{W})$
item.description:
Author(s): Jin-Han Tan, Yu-Lin Han, Shu-Xiang Qiao, Meng-Meng Zheng, Ping Zhang, and Hong-Yan Lu

Double-layer honeycomb (DLHC) materials exhibit tunable electronic properties and enhanced stability over single-layer/bulk honeycomb forms, but existing experimental research has predominantly focused on semiconducting systems, while theoretical studies on superconductivity remain sparse. Through f…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224512] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jin-Han Tan, Yu-Lin Han, Shu-Xiang Qiao, Meng-Meng Zheng, Ping Zhang, and Hong-Yan Lu

Double-layer honeycomb (DLHC) materials exhibit tunable electronic properties and enhanced stability over single-layer/bulk honeycomb forms, but existing experimental research has predominantly focused on semiconducting systems, while theoretical studies on superconductivity remain sparse. Through f…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224512] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.dc:title: Superconductivity and band topology in the double-layer honeycomb structure compounds ${X}_{2}{\mathrm{C}}_{2}$ $(X=\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{Ta}, \mathrm{W})$

item.title: Enhanced superconducting correlations in the Emery model and its connections to strange metallic transport and normal state coherence
item.description:
Author(s): Sijia Zhao, Rong Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Jixun K. Ding, Tianyi Liu, Brian Moritz, Edwin W. Huang, and Thomas P. Devereaux

Numerical evidence for superconductivity in the single-band Hubbard model is elusive or ambiguous despite extensive study, raising the question of whether the single-band Hubbard model is a faithful low-energy effective model for cuprates and whether explicitly including the oxygen ions will recover…

item.title: Mixed-valence Mott insulator and composite excitation in twisted bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Jing-Yu Zhao, Boran Zhou, and Ya-Hui Zhang

The interplay of strong correlation and flat topological bands is a central problem in moiré systems such as magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (TBG). In this work, we construct the ground state wave function and exotic excitations for correlated phases in TBG at even integer fillings. At a fillin…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235144] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025

item.title: Magnetoelectric response arising from Zeeman quantum geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Jie Cao, Fenghua Qi, Yuanyuan Xiang, and Guojun Jin

The quantum metric is a foundational quantity for Bloch states, yet it has garnered far less attention than its counterpart, i.e., the Berry curvature. Here, we investigate the magnetoelectric effects using Zeeman quantum geometry, which is defined in [Phys. Rev. Lett. 134, 116301 (2025)], aiming to…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235145] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025

Here, we investigate the magnetoelectric effects using Zeeman quantum geometry, which is defined in [Phys. Rev. Lett. 134, 116301 (2025)], aiming to…

item.title: Theory of nonresonant Raman scattering from electrons in nodal and flat bands
item.description:
Author(s): Predrag Nikolić

Raman scattering is emerging as a surprising probe of electron topology in quantum materials. It has been used recently to detect and characterize a topological phase transition that accompanies the magnetic transition in ${\mathrm{Nd}}_{2}{\mathrm{Ir}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$. Here we present a theory…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235146] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Predrag Nikolić

Raman scattering is emerging as a surprising probe of electron topology in quantum materials. It has been used recently to detect and characterize a topological phase transition that accompanies the magnetic transition in Nd2Ir2O7. Here we present a theory of Raman scattering from nodal electrons wi…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235146] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025

item.title: Giant enhancement of Rashba spin splitting and anisotropy in thermodynamically stable Janus $\mathrm{Ta}QX$ monolayers $(Q=\mathrm{O},\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{S};\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}X=\mathrm{F},\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{Cl},\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{Br})$
item.description:

Structural symmetry breaking in two-dimensional systems introduces additional electronic degrees of freedom, fostering exotic quantum phenomena such as the Rashba effect, magnetic anisotropy, and nontrivial topological states, thereby advancing their relevance in spintronic, valleytronic, and quantu…

item.description:

In this study we present a thorough theoretical and numerical investigation of the unified optical conductivity response in two-dimensional (2D) lattice systems that transition between the Lieb and kagome lattices through a tunable shear angle, $ϕ$. This continuous transformation enables us to analy…

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Quantum oscillations reveal sixfold fermions in cubic $β\text{−}{\mathrm{PtBi}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): E. F. Bavaro, J. Castro, V. Vildosola, J. I. Facio, and V. F. Correa

High-resolution de Haas–-van Alphen measurements on cubic β-PtBi2, combined with fully relativistic density functional calculations, reveal quantum oscillations arising from a symmetry-enforced sixfold band touching at the Brillouin zone corner. Three small electron pockets derived from this degeneracy, lying ~25 meV below the Fermi level, are identified through their low oscillation frequencies and angular dependence. These results provide bulk evidence for sixfold fermions in β-PtBi2, bridging ARPES observations and theory and establishing a robust platform for multifold quasiparticles.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245136] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): E. F. Bavaro, J. Castro, V. Vildosola, J. I. Facio, and V. F. Correa

High-resolution de Haas–-van Alphen measurements on cubic β-PtBi2, combined with fully relativistic density functional calculations, reveal quantum oscillations arising from a symmetry-enforced sixfold band touching at the Brillouin zone corner. Three small electron pockets derived from this degeneracy, lying ~25 meV below the Fermi level, are identified through their low oscillation frequencies and angular dependence. These results provide bulk evidence for sixfold fermions in β-PtBi2, bridging ARPES observations and theory and establishing a robust platform for multifold quasiparticles.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245136] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantum oscillations reveal sixfold fermions in cubic $β\text{−}{\mathrm{PtBi}}_{2}$

item.description:
Author(s): Chakradhar Rangi, Juana Moreno, and Ka-Ming Tam

Non-Hermitian quantum systems, characterized by their ability to model open systems with gain and loss, have unveiled striking phenomena such as the non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE), where eigenstates localize at boundaries under open boundary conditions. While extensively studied in noninteracting …


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245137] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:

Non-Hermitian quantum systems, characterized by their ability to model open systems with gain and loss, have unveiled striking phenomena such as the non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE), where eigenstates localize at boundaries under open boundary conditions. While extensively studied in noninteracting …


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245137] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025

item.title: Antichiral edge states and Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces in a two-dimensional proximity-induced superconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriel F. Rodríguez Ruiz, Juan Herrera Mateos, Leandro Tosi, Christoph Strunk, Carlos Balseiro, and Liliana Arrachea

We show that a magnetic field parallel to the plane of a two-dimensional electron gas with Rashba spin-orbit coupling in proximity to a superconductor leads to a topological phase in coexistence with a single pair of Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces. This phase hosts antichiral edge states of copropagating…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, L241409] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriel F. Rodríguez Ruiz, Juan Herrera Mateos, Leandro Tosi, Christoph Strunk, Carlos Balseiro, and Liliana Arrachea

We show that a magnetic field parallel to the plane of a two-dimensional electron gas with Rashba spin-orbit coupling in proximity to a superconductor leads to a topological phase in coexistence with a single pair of Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces. This phase hosts antichiral edge states of copropagating…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, L241409] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025

The studying of the interplay between magnetism and topology is crucial for understanding the fundamental physics of quantum materials and for developing next-generation electronic devices. Here, we investigate the emergence of half-metallic hybrid nodal chains (HMHNC) in ${\mathrm{YMnO}}_{3}$, reve…

item.title: Theory of circular dichroism in resonant inelastic x-ray scattering
item.description:
Author(s): M. Furo, A. Hariki, and J. Kuneš

We analyze circular dichroism (CD) in resonant inelastic x-ray scattering (RIXS) in magnetic materials. We define RIXS-CD as the difference between scattering amplitudes for the right- and left-circularly polarized incoming photons and unpolarized (total) outgoing photons. We employ the impurity app…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214429] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025

Author(s): M. Furo, A. Hariki, and J. Kuneš

We analyze circular dichroism (CD) in resonant inelastic x-ray scattering (RIXS) in magnetic materials. We define RIXS-CD as the difference between scattering amplitudes for the right- and left-circularly polarized incoming photons and unpolarized (total) outgoing photons. We employ the impurity app…

item.title: Majoranas with a twist: Tunable Majorana zero modes in altermagnetic heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Andreas Hadjipaschalis, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, and Jennifer Cano

Altermagnetism provides new routes to realize Majorana zero modes with vanishing net magnetization. We consider a recently proposed heterostructure consisting of a semiconducting wire on top of an altermagnet and with proximity-induced superconductivity. We demonstrate that rotating the wire serves …

We consider a recently proposed heterostructure consisting of a semiconducting wire on top of an altermagnet and with proximity-induced superconductivity. We demonstrate that rotating the wire serves …

Metal-doped borophenes offer a versatile platform for realizing topological superconductivity and investigating exotic quantum states. In this study, through extensive structural searches, we identify the $t\text{-Be}{\mathrm{B}}_{3}$ monolayer and its stacked bilayer as the lowest-energy configurat…

item.description:

Quasi-two-dimensional (2D) layered structured BiTe, a member of the infinitely adaptive series ${({\mathrm{Bi}}_{2})}_{m}{({\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3})}_{n}$, is an efficient thermoelectric material and a dual topological insulator at ambient pressure. This undergoes pressure-induced sequenti…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214511] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:

Quasi-two-dimensional (2D) layered structured BiTe, a member of the infinitely adaptive series (Bi2)m(Bi2Te3)n, is an efficient thermoelectric material and a dual topological insulator at ambient pressure. This undergoes pressure-induced sequential structural transitions into various 3D network stru…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214511] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Coexistence of static and dynamic local magnetic fields in the distorted honeycomb lattice antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Co}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{8}$
item.description:
Author(s): J. Khatua, Suheon Lee, M. Pregelj, Samiul Sk, S. K. Panda, Bassam Hitti, Gerald Morris, I. da Silva, Kwang-Yong Choi, and P. Khuntia

The bipartite lattice based honeycomb quantum magnets, characterized by low coordination numbers, provide a viable route to realize exotic quantum phenomena due to an intricate interplay between competing magnetic interactions, spin-orbit coupling, and crystal electric fields. Here, the authors demonstrate the coexistence of static and dynamic local magnetic fields governed by intraplanar further-neighbor interactions and weak interplanar antiferromagnetic coupling in a 3d transition metal ion based frustrated honeycomb lattice antiferromagnet stabilizing in the XY universality class.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224425] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. Khatua, Suheon Lee, M. Pregelj, Samiul Sk, S. K. Panda, Bassam Hitti, Gerald Morris, I. da Silva, Kwang-Yong Choi, and P. Khuntia

The bipartite lattice based honeycomb quantum magnets, characterized by low coordination numbers, provide a viable route to realize exotic quantum phenomena due to an intricate interplay between competing magnetic interactions, spin-orbit coupling, and crystal electric fields. Here, the authors demonstrate the coexistence of static and dynamic local magnetic fields governed by intraplanar further-neighbor interactions and weak interplanar antiferromagnetic coupling in a 3d transition metal ion based frustrated honeycomb lattice antiferromagnet stabilizing in the XY universality class.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224425] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: Coexistence of static and dynamic local magnetic fields in the distorted honeycomb lattice antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Co}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{8}$

We study the emergence of topological order in a…

However, direct electronic structure studies are still lacking. Here, we have investigated the electronic structure of ${\mathrm{Nb}}_…

item.title: Helical Fermi arc in altermagnetic Weyl semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): Yu-Hao Wan, Cheng-Ming Miao, Peng-Yi Liu, and Qing-Feng Sun

We investigate the topological properties of modified Dirac Hamiltonians with an altermagnetic mass term and reveal a mechanism for realizing altermagnetic Weyl semimetals. Unlike the conventional Wilson mass, the altermagnetic mass drives direct transitions between nontrivial Chern phases of opposi…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235411] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Hao Wan, Cheng-Ming Miao, Peng-Yi Liu, and Qing-Feng Sun

We investigate the topological properties of modified Dirac Hamiltonians with an altermagnetic mass term and reveal a mechanism for realizing altermagnetic Weyl semimetals. Unlike the conventional Wilson mass, the altermagnetic mass drives direct transitions between nontrivial Chern phases of opposi…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235411] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: Helical Fermi arc in altermagnetic Weyl semimetal
item.dc:creator: Yu-Hao Wan, Cheng-Ming Miao, Peng-Yi Liu, and Qing-Feng Sun
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Magnetic control of quantum vacuum friction near a graphene sheet
item.description:
Author(s): Kaipeng Liu, Shiwei Dai, and Lixin Ge

The authors investigate here quantum vacuum friction acting on a rotating nanosphere near a graphene sheet and demonstrate that the friction force can be substantially tuned by a magnetic field. This tunability originates from the mode coupling between graphene’s magnetoplasmon polaritons and the localized surface phonon polaritons in nanospheres. Interestingly, the authors reveal that the axial Casimir force—a dissipationless friction—exhibits quantized steplike features as the magnetic field and/or the chemical potential increases. This phenomenon is attributed to the quantum Hall effect of graphene at low temperatures.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235412] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kaipeng Liu, Shiwei Dai, and Lixin Ge

The authors investigate here quantum vacuum friction acting on a rotating nanosphere near a graphene sheet and demonstrate that the friction force can be substantially tuned by a magnetic field. This tunability originates from the mode coupling between graphene’s magnetoplasmon polaritons and the localized surface phonon polaritons in nanospheres. Interestingly, the authors reveal that the axial Casimir force—a dissipationless friction—exhibits quantized steplike features as the magnetic field and/or the chemical potential increases. This phenomenon is attributed to the quantum Hall effect of graphene at low temperatures.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235412] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: Magnetic control of quantum vacuum friction near a graphene sheet
item.dc:creator: Kaipeng Liu, Shiwei Dai, and Lixin Ge
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Many-body electronic structure in the pyrochlore superconductor $\mathrm{Cs}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$ and spin-liquid candidate $\mathrm{P}{\mathrm{r}}_{2}\mathrm{I}{\mathrm{r}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$
item.description:
Author(s): Wei Song et al.

The authors present here the first atomic-scale investigation of the pyrochlore superconductor CsBi2 and the spin liquid Pr2Ir2O7 using scanning tunneling microscopy at 300 mK. The many-body electronic structure, including strong-coupling superconductivity and homogeneous Kondo-lattice resonance, are imaged. They further find unusual excitations, including vortex core states with threefold symmetry and site-dependent Zeeman splitting of the Kondo resonance, which provide atomic insight into the interplay between geometrical frustration and many-body phenomena in this pyrochlore lattice.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245131] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei Song et al.

The authors present here the first atomic-scale investigation of the pyrochlore superconductor CsBi2 and the spin liquid Pr2Ir2O7 using scanning tunneling microscopy at 300 mK. The many-body electronic structure, including strong-coupling superconductivity and homogeneous Kondo-lattice resonance, are imaged. They further find unusual excitations, including vortex core states with threefold symmetry and site-dependent Zeeman splitting of the Kondo resonance, which provide atomic insight into the interplay between geometrical frustration and many-body phenomena in this pyrochlore lattice.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245131] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: Many-body electronic structure in the pyrochlore superconductor $\mathrm{Cs}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$ and spin-liquid candidate $\mathrm{P}{\mathrm{r}}_{2}\mathrm{I}{\mathrm{r}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$
item.dc:creator: Wei Song et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Perturbative renormalization group approach to magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene using topological heavy fermion model
item.description:
Author(s): Yi Huang, Yang-Zhi Chou, and Sankar Das Sarma

The authors present here a renormalization group analysis of the topological heavy fermion model in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene. In the energy regime where interactions and hybridization compete, renormalization drives the system toward the chiral limit, lowering the effective interaction strength relative to hybridization. This work bridges the Kondo-like and Mott semimetal scenarios, providing a foundation for modeling the low-energy physics of strongly correlated moiré flat bands.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245132] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi Huang, Yang-Zhi Chou, and Sankar Das Sarma

The authors present here a renormalization group analysis of the topological heavy fermion model in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene. In the energy regime where interactions and hybridization compete, renormalization drives the system toward the chiral limit, lowering the effective interaction strength relative to hybridization. This work bridges the Kondo-like and Mott semimetal scenarios, providing a foundation for modeling the low-energy physics of strongly correlated moiré flat bands.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245132] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: Perturbative renormalization group approach to magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene using topological heavy fermion model
